danger_signals.visual_form: Hostile samurai and ninja sprites occupying screen space
danger_signals.meaning: Direct combat threat; player must engage or evade

danger_signals.visual_form: Sprite-based slash and projectile movements from adversaries
danger_signals.meaning: Imminent damage; player must respond with blocking/dodging or counterattack

danger_signals.visual_form: Increasingly fortified environmental layouts across five stages
danger_signals.meaning: Escalating difficulty and enemy density; political/military power concentration

danger_signals.visual_form: Warlord Yoshi and samurai Yuki as distinct, larger sprite entities
danger_signals.meaning: Major threat encounter; story-critical antagonist confrontation

danger_signals.visual_form: Sprite color reversion from powered-up states (green/orange) back to red clothes
danger_signals.meaning: Player vulnerability increased; loss of temporary stat advantages

safety_signals.visual_form: Collectible item that changes player clothing color (red → green → orange progression)
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary invulnerability or stat boost; progression marker and risk mitigation

safety_signals.visual_form: NPC character acquisition and stage progression (rescues occur 2-3 times across game)
safety_signals.meaning: Story progression; safe checkpoint equivalent in rescue narrative structure

safety_signals.visual_form: Background/environment palette shifts from summer → fall → winter → summer cycle
safety_signals.meaning: Level completion; natural cycle reset and safe state transition

safety_signals.visual_form: Cleared platforms and pathways with no active enemies
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary safety window; opportunity for resource gathering or positioning

faction_indicators.color_identity: Red (base clothing color), upgradeable to Green or Orange
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Kodachi shortsword and unlimited shuriken as class weapons
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Player sprite uses ninja silhouette; contrasts with samurai enemy designs
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Forest and secret passageway stages align with ninja stealth traditions; lighter, mobile aesthetic

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Princess Kirihime as symbolic objective; rescue mission defines faction loyalty
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Fortress wall and castle environments represent Shogun's seat of power; fortification density increases as player approaches

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Warlord Yoshi and samurai Yuki as named antagonists; samurai armor/weapon sprites differentiate from ninja
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Red ninja enemies (distinct from player), monks with fire-breathing abilities, generic samurai soldiers
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Castle and fortress ownership; concentration of armed forces in late-game stages signals faction control

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Open terrain with sparse platforms; natural background elements; lighter enemy density
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Lower enemy spawn rate; combat encounters are manageable; tutorial-like pacing

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Enclosed, vertical architecture; walls and narrow passages dominate composition; environmental hazards implied
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Increased enemy frequency; more aggressive multi-directional attacks; platforming complexity rises

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Heavily fortified interior spaces; ornate architecture; dense sprite clustering on screen
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Maximum enemy density; boss encounters; mandatory rescue sequences; heightened difficulty and visual clutter

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Seasonal palette shift (summer → fall → winter → summer cycle reset); stage restart with visual differentiation
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Level replay with difficulty scaling; enemy placement variations; narrative loop indicates continued threat

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Player clothing color change (red → green → orange); visual stat/ability upgrade indicator
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Temporary enhanced attack or speed; invulnerability during powered-up state; reversion to base state on damage

environmental_storytelling.example: Progression from forest → secret passageway → fortress wall → castle interior narratively encodes player penetration into enemy territory and movement toward power concentration
Open natural terrain evolves into fortified military architecture, visually conveying the warlord's defensive posture and political dominance over the region
Summer-to-winter-to-summer loops suggest time passage, multiple rescue attempts, and cyclical nature of the conflict; reinforces narrative stakes through environmental change

Red ninja, samurai soldiers, and fire-breathing monks scattered across stages suggest heterogeneous enemy faction composition; diversified military forces indicate warlord's resources and organization
Princess Kirihime's capture location (castle depths) visually encodes narrative priority and spatial hierarchy; rescue acts as environmental milestone achieving victory state

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Standard attack speed and shuriken size; baseline combat capability; vulnerability to damage
Enhanced attack speed or larger projectiles; invulnerability or damage reduction; color change signals enhanced state to player
Revert to red clothes; lose stat bonuses; survive the hit rather than lose life; signals temporary protection mechanic exhaustion
Sprite animations suggest ready combat stance; jump and slash patterns indicate aggressive patrol behavior
Synchronized attack animations; multi-directional strikes; crowd behavior shows coordinated pressure tactics
Larger sprite scaling; distinct attack patterns; positioned as narrative encounter rather than generic enemy; behavior complexity suggests tactical opponent
Static positioning in stage end-point; no independent movement; visual marker for stage completion objective
Removal from screen; trigger stage progression and seasonal palette shift; behavioral change indicates world-state transition

notes: The Legend of Kage communicates world-state primarily through environmental architecture escalation and seasonal cycling rather than explicit UI elements. Danger is signaled through enemy density and sprite positioning. Faction identity relies on character sprite differentiation (ninja vs. samurai aesthetics) and stage geography (natural vs. fortified spaces). The game lacks explicit political narrative elements; world-state communication is implicit through mission structure (princess rescue) and enemy hierarchy (generic soldiers → named bosses). Power-up color changes serve dual function as gameplay mechanic and visual feedback system. NES version maintains arcade design philosophy of visual clarity within technical constraints.
